descriptions: Ascone is a satellite cosca of Bellocco.
edges.evidence_quote: Mantengono stretti legami con la 'ndrina dei Pesce , con i Varone e la cosca satellite degli Ascone .

Cártel de Colima.description: Mexican criminal organization based in Colima, founded in 1988 by the Amezcua Contreras family. Specialized in methamphetamine production and trafficking, with operations in Mexico and the United States. It was dismantled in 2005, with some members joining the Jalisco New Generation Cartel.

descriptions: Cártel de Colima is allied with Cartel de Guadalajara.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Cártel de Sinaloa , Cártel del Milenio , Cartel de Guadalajara .

descriptions: Cártel de Colima considers Cártel de Juárez an enemy.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Cártel de Tijuana , Cártel de Juárez .

descriptions: Cártel de Colima is allied with Cártel del Milenio.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Cártel de Sinaloa , Cártel del Milenio , Cartel de Guadalajara .

descriptions: Cártel de Colima became a branch of Cártel de Sinaloa.
edges.evidence_quote: El cartel de Colima se ha convertido en una rama del Cartel de Sinaloa

descriptions: Cártel de Colima considers Cártel de Tijuana an enemy.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Cártel de Tijuana , Cártel de Juárez .

descriptions: CJNG is linked to the Colima Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Dicho grupo se ha vinculado con el grupo criminal de los hermanos Amezcua Contreras, conocido como el Cártel de Colima

'Ndrina Cordì.description: The Cordì are a 'ndrina (mafia clan) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Locri, Calabria, Italy. They have a strong presence in Portigliola, Gerace, and Antonimina, and are historical rivals of the Cataldo clan. Active since at least the 1960s, they are involved in drug trafficking and have operations in Marche, Emilia, and Rome. The clan has been engaged in a long-running feud with the Cataldo family, resulting in over 30 deaths by 2005.

descriptions: Cataldo and Cordì are in a blood feud.
edges.evidence_quote: involved in a long blood feud with the Cordì 'ndrina

descriptions: Cataldo and Cordi were opposing sides in the Faida di Locri.
edges.evidence_quote: Da allora le cosche Cataldo e Cordi si sono combattute a più riprese fino agli arresti compiuti nel 1999

descriptions: The Cataldo are historical rivals of the Cordì.
edges.evidence_quote: storici rivali dei Cordì

descriptions: After a truce, they formed an alliance for joint crime management.
edges.evidence_quote: they formed a strong alliance aimed at a joint management of the crime business

descriptions: Cordì 'ndrina is a sub-unit of the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: The Cordì 'ndrina is a clan of the 'Ndrangheta

descriptions: Cordì and Cataldo clans were in a violent feud.
edges.evidence_quote: the beginning of a long blood feud with the Cataldo 'ndrina

descriptions: The Cordì are historical rivals of the Cataldo.
edges.evidence_quote: storici rivali dei Cataldo

descriptions: The feud with the Cataldo resumed in 1993.
edges.evidence_quote: Dal 1993 rincomincia la faida con i Cataldo.

descriptions: After a long feud, the two clans formed an alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: the Cordì and Cataldo clans signed a peace after a bitter feud that lasted for 40 years causing dozens of deaths. They formed a strong alliance

descriptions: A peace was signed with the Cataldo in 2010.
edges.evidence_quote: Nel 2010 dopo ben oltre quarant'anni dall'omicidio di Domenico Cordì nel 1967 sembra si sia siglata una pace con i Cataldo.

descriptions: Costa 'ndrina is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Cataldo 'ndrina , Mazzaferro 'ndrina , Costa 'ndrina, Ierinò 'ndrina

descriptions: In Locri, the Cordì are allied with the Floccari.
edges.evidence_quote: A Locri sono alleati con i Floccari.

descriptions: Operation Leone arrested members of Iamonte and Cordì together, implying a cooperative relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 3 febbraio 2010 i Carabinieri, durante l'operazione Leone , arrestano 67 persone che gestiva un traffico di immigrati, tra cui esponenti degli Iamonte e dei Cordì insieme a persone di origine indiana.

descriptions: Ierinò 'ndrina is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Cataldo 'ndrina , Mazzaferro 'ndrina , Costa 'ndrina, Ierinò 'ndrina

descriptions: Operation Domino targeted Mamone alongside Cordì, implying a cooperative relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: 4 giugno 2007 - Operazione Domino contro i Nesci-Montagnese, i Mamone, i Morabito e i Cordì.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Cataldo 'ndrina , Mazzaferro 'ndrina , Costa 'ndrina, Ierinò 'ndrina

descriptions: Operation Domino targeted Morabito alongside Cordì, implying a cooperative relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: 4 giugno 2007 - Operazione Domino contro i Nesci-Montagnese, i Mamone, i Morabito e i Cordì.

descriptions: Mimmo Crea favored Morabito-Zavettieri, Cordì, and Talia, implying a cooperative relationship among them.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 22 dicembre 2010 l'ex consigliere della Regione Calabria Mimmo Crea viene condannato in primo grado a 11 anni di carcere accusato di concorso esterno in associazione mafiosa favorendo i Morabito-Zavettieri , i Cordì e i Talia.

descriptions: Operation Domino targeted Nesci-Montagnese, Mamone, Morabito, and Cordì together, implying a cooperative relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: 4 giugno 2007 - Operazione Domino contro i Nesci-Montagnese, i Mamone, i Morabito e i Cordì.

descriptions: Mimmo Crea favored Talia alongside Cordì, implying a cooperative relationship.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Commisso 'ndrina , Aquino 'ndrina, Ursino 'ndrina

descriptions: Aquino 'ndrina is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Commisso 'ndrina , Aquino 'ndrina, Ursino 'ndrina

descriptions: Commisso 'ndrina is listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Commisso 'ndrina , Aquino 'ndrina, Ursino 'ndrina

descriptions: Morabito killed Domenico Cordì for bypassing them in cigarette smuggling, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 23 giugno 1967 avviene la strage di Locri: sono assassinati il boss Domenico Cordì, Carmelo Siciliano e Vincenzo Saraceno. L'obiettivo è di punire Domenico Cordì per aver commerciato, a titolo personale, un carico di sigarette di contrabbando scavalcando la cosca Morabito.

descriptions: The Ursino are allied with the Cordì.
edges.evidence_quote: Gli Ursino sono una potente 'ndrina di Gioiosa Jonica , alleata degli Aquino di Marina di Gioiosa Jonica e dei Cordì di Locri

edges.evidence_quote: un grupo conocido como la Organización Herrera transporta múltiples toneladas de cocaína desde Sudamérica a Guatemala y Honduras en nombre del Cartel de Sinaloa.

descriptions: Ghetto Brothers called a peace meeting attended by Young Sinners.
edges.evidence_quote: It took place on December 8, 1971, and was attended by two hundred representatives from dozens of Bronx gangs, including the Black Pearls, Black Spades, Dirty Dozens, Dutchmen, Latin Spades, Liberated Panthers, Magnificent Seven, Peacemakers, Royal Javelins, Savage Skulls, Seven Immortals, Turbans, and Young Sinners, together with police and city officials.

Lucerne Street Doggz.description: A street gang based in the Lucerne Street area of Mattapan, Boston, founded in 2003. Primarily African American, with an estimated 50+ members. Engaged in drug trafficking, robbery, and murder. In 2006, the gang was involved in 37 shootings, accounting for 10% of all shootings in Boston that year.

descriptions: Mascot Street Killas are listed as allies of Lucerne Street Doggz.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Mascot Street Killas

descriptions: Morse Street County are listed as rivals of Lucerne Street Doggz.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Norfolk street Bulls, Morse Street County

descriptions: Norfolk Street Bulls are listed as rivals of Lucerne Street Doggz.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Norfolk street Bulls, Morse Street County

Caborca Cartel.description: Mexican criminal organization based in Caborca, Sonora, founded in 2019 by veteran drug lord Rafael Caro Quintero. It concentrates drug trafficking operations in the border area and has a bloody dispute with Los Salazar, the armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel. The group allied with the Juárez Cartel to dispute territories in Sonora and Chihuahua. Despite Caro Quintero's capture in 2022, the cartel remains active.

descriptions: The Caborca Cartel is in a violent conflict with Los Salazar.
edges.evidence_quote: At present, the group has a bloody dispute with the Los Salazar criminal cell, identified as the armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel.

descriptions: The Caborca Cartel lists the Jalisco New Generation Cartel as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Juárez Cartel , Jalisco New Generation Cartel

descriptions: The Caborca Cartel formed an alliance with the Juárez Cartel to fight for territory.
edges.evidence_quote: The group allied with the Juárez Cartel to dispute the areas of Sonora and Chihuahua

descriptions: The Caborca Cartel is in conflict with Los Chapitos over territory.
edges.evidence_quote: the main objective of Caro Quintero has been to dispute the territory of Sonora with the faction of Los Chapitos and Los Salazar

descriptions: The Caborca Cartel lists the Sinaloa Cartel as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mexico , United States , Sinaloa Cartel

edges.evidence_quote: fundaron su propia organización conocida como el Cártel de Caborca . En la actualidad el grupo tiene una disputa con la célula delictiva de Los Salazar

descriptions: Both clubs emerged as outlaw motorcycle clubs after the 1947 Hollister Rally.
edges.evidence_quote: This led to the beginning of the highly visible and structured 1% or outlaw motorcycle clubs , along with the Boozefighters MC when the AMA forbade club members to participate in AMA events unless they took off their patches.

Moccia clan.description: The Clan Moccia is the oldest and most powerful Camorra organization operating in the lower Liburia area, particularly in Afragola, Casoria, Arzano, Caivano and neighboring municipalities north and northeast of Naples. Founded by Gennaro Moccia in the 1960s, it was later led by his wife Anna Mazza, the first woman in Italy to face criminal proceedings for mafia crimes. The clan has a confederative structure with local groups and maintains a hegemonic presence in the territory, confirmed by multiple judicial investigations.

descriptions: Moccia clan is a Camorra clan, part of the Camorra organization.
edges.evidence_quote: According to Misso, the real power of the organization is now in the hands of the Licciardi clan , Mallardo clan , Moccia clan and Contini clan .

descriptions: Moccia clan is a constituent clan of the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: the Moccia clan, from Afragola, named after Gennaro Moccia;

descriptions: Aldo Ferrucci, a Casalesi affiliate, was also linked to the Moccia clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Prima dell'altro arresto scaturito da un'inchiesta sul clan Moccia, al quale Ferrucci era anche legato

descriptions: Moccia clan is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Moccia clan

descriptions: Alfieri clan allied with Moccia clan.
edges.evidence_quote: He also made new alliances with the ... Moccia clan from Afragola

descriptions: Clan Alfieri allied with the Moccia clan.
edges.evidence_quote: con i Moccia di Afragola

descriptions: Clan Cava, Clan Russo, and Clan Moccia plotted together against Antonio Guerriero.
edges.evidence_quote: Stando a quanto dichiarato dal pentito di Nola Ciro Di Domenico, a tramare contro Guerriero furono tre clan (i Cava, i Russo e i Moccia ) .

descriptions: Members of Clan Cava, Clan Moccia, and Clan Russo were arrested together for usury and extortion, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: In data 17 giugno 2011 ... sottoponevano a fermo di Polizia Giudiziaria 5 pregiudicati esponenti dei clan Cava di Quindici , Moccia di Afragola e Russo di Nola , ritenuti responsabili dei reati di usura ed estorsione aggravata

descriptions: Clan Magliulo and Clan Moccia were in a bloody feud over a construction contract in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni novanta furono protagonisti di una faida sanguinosa contro il clan Moccia: le due famiglie si contendevano l'appalto per la costruzione dell'attuale Rione Salicelle.

descriptions: Clan Russo and Clan Moccia plotted together with Clan Cava against Antonio Guerriero.
edges.evidence_quote: Stando a quanto dichiarato dal pentito di Nola Ciro Di Domenico, a tramare contro Guerriero furono tre clan (i Cava, i Russo e i Moccia ) .

edges.evidence_quote: With the downfall of the Magliulo clan, the Moccia clan shot down one of the pillars of the Alfieri clan in the northeast area of Naples.

edges.evidence_quote: probabilmente da un gruppo di sicari al soldo dei rivali dei Moccia, ossia i clan Giugliano e Magliulo, all'epoca impegnati in una faida proprio con i Moccia

descriptions: Magliulo clan killed Vincenzo Moccia, leading to a war that Moccia clan won.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1987, Mazza's favorite son Vincenzo Moccia, called Angioletto , was killed by the rival Magliulo clan, that led to another bloody war between the clans, the Moccias came out victorious.

descriptions: Clan Magliulo was a rival in a feud with Moccia.
edges.evidence_quote: probabilmente da un gruppo di sicari al soldo dei rivali dei Moccia, ossia i clan Giugliano e Magliulo, all'epoca impegnati in una faida proprio con i Moccia

descriptions: Clan Moccia replaced Clan Magliulo.
edges.evidence_quote: Ha preso il posto del clan Magliulo

descriptions: Clan Moccia had a feud with Clan Natale over drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: non avendo sviluppato il redditizio traffico di droga, avrebbe provocato una faida con il clan Natale

edges.evidence_quote: i Moccia, anche in collaborazione con il clan capeggiato da Michele Senese

descriptions: Moccia clan was a loyal member of the Nuova Famiglia alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The Moccia clan was always a loyal member of the Nuova Famiglia in the war against the Nuova Camorra Organizzata led by Raffaele Cutolo.

descriptions: The Moccia clan was part of the Nuova Famiglia confederation.
edges.evidence_quote: The confederation included: - Angelo Moccia, boss of the Moccia clan

descriptions: BAMOSD receives support from SCAPO and NDDSC.
edges.evidence_quote: recibe apoyo de la SCAPO y del NDDSC

descriptions: A member of Pelle 'ndrina (Giuseppe Vottari) is allied with the Nirta clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Giuseppe Vottari, known as "U massaru" or "capobastone", allied with the Nirta clan since the early seventies.

descriptions: The two clans were opposed in the first faida of Ercolano.
edges.evidence_quote: La prima faida di Ercolano vede contrapposti il clan degli Esposito contro il clan degli Ascione.

descriptions: Ascione and Esposito were in open war during the first faida.
edges.evidence_quote: Tra i due clan è ormai guerra dichiarata.

descriptions: Clan Mariano (remnants) allied with Esposito, Lepre, Elia, and Terracciano after Marco Mariano's release.
edges.evidence_quote: I superstiti del clan starebbero tentando di riorganizzarsi intorno al fratello dello storico capo dei Picuozzi, unendosi in alleanza con i clan degli Esposito, dei Lepre della zona del Cavone e degli Elia del Pallonetto di Santa Lucia, a questo sodalizio si sarebbe unito anche il clan di Salvatore Terracciano (detto o'Nirone) dei Quartieri Spagnoli, storico affiliato dei Picuozzi.

Los Rojos Cartel.description: Mexican criminal organization that split from the Beltran-Leyva Cartel in 2009, based in Guerrero and Morelos. Engages in drug trafficking, money laundering, extortion, robbery, murder, arms trafficking, prostitution, and kidnapping. Known for financing political candidates and violent territorial disputes with rival cartels. Still active as of 2021.

descriptions: Los Rojos Cartel were formerly enforcers of the Beltrán-Leyva Organization.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Rojos Cartel, in Guerrero. These are former enforcers of the Beltrán-Leyva brothers, apparently independent and dominating much of Guerrero.

descriptions: Los Rojos Cartel split from Beltran-Leyva Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: The Los Rojos Cartel ... is a Mexican criminal organization that emerged as a split from the Beltran-Leyva Cartel

descriptions: Guerreros Unidos is listed as an ally of Los Rojos in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Guerreros Unidos

descriptions: Jalisco New Generation Cartel is listed as a rival of Los Rojos in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mexico , Jalisco New Generation Cartel , La Familia Michoacana , Guerreros Unidos .

descriptions: Los Rojos fought La Familia Michoacana over territory in 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: The authorities confirmed that the cause of the confrontation was due to a dispute over territory between Los Rojos and cells belonging to La Familia Michoacana.

descriptions: Mandiant linked Evil Corp to threat actor UNC2165.
edges.evidence_quote: Mandiant also linked the group to threat actor UNC2165.

descriptions: MNLA and KNLA have had minor skirmishes.
edges.evidence_quote: It signed the Nationwide Ceasefire Agreement in 2015 and has had minor skirmishes with the Karen National Liberation Army (KNLA).

descriptions: MNLA is the armed wing of NMSP.
edges.evidence_quote: The Mon people have sought self-determination since Myanmar gained independence in 1948, initially under the Mon People's Front and from 1962 through the New Mon State Party (NMSP). The Mon National Liberation Army (MNLA) has been fighting government forces since 1949.

descriptions: Infobox lists SHARP as an enemy.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: SHARP

descriptions: The Aryan Brotherhood lists SHARP as an enemy.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Hermandad Aria de Texas, Black Guerrilla Family, Black P. Stones, D.C. Blacks, Bloods, Crips, SHARP, Nuestra Familia, las Panteras Negras, Liga de defensa Judía

descriptions: Al-Nusra opposed Shabiha.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Shabiha

descriptions: Borghetto is a cosca close to Libri.
edges.evidence_quote: I Borghetto, gli Zindato e i Caridi sono cosche vicine ai Libri.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Bunnikside

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Bunnikside

The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of.description: A far-right survivalist anti-government militia advocating Christian Identity, active in the United States during the 1970s and early 1980s. It developed from a Baptist congregation founded in 1971 in Pontiac, Missouri, and operated a compound in northern Arkansas known as 'the Farm'. The group was dissolved after a 1985 siege and subsequent convictions of its leaders.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
descriptions: The CSA had links to the Aryan Brotherhood.
edges.evidence_quote: The CSA had links to other radical organizations, including the Aryan Brotherhood, the Mountain Church, and The Order

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
descriptions: CSA leader Ellison had close ties to Aryan Nations.
edges.evidence_quote: Ellison had close ties to the Ku Klux Klan and Aryan Nations, based in Hayden Lake, Idaho, and led by Butler

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
descriptions: CSA is affiliated with Aryan Nations.
edges.evidence_quote: Affiliations: The Order , Aryan Nations , Elohim City

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord

edges.evidence_quote: Affiliations: The Order, Aryan Nations, Elohim City

edges.evidence_quote: Ellison had close ties to the Ku Klux Klan and Aryan Nations

edges.target: Mountain Church of Jesus Christ the Savior
descriptions: The CSA had links to the Mountain Church.
edges.evidence_quote: The CSA had links to other radical organizations, including the Aryan Brotherhood, the Mountain Church, and The Order

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
descriptions: The Pagans Motorcycle Club provided training to the CSA in exchange for weapons.
edges.evidence_quote: the Pagans Motorcycle Club provided the CSA with training in booby trap devices and survival techniques in return for weapons and ammunition.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
descriptions: CSA's actions were linked to events involving a Posse Comitatus member, but no direct relationship between the organizations is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: The death knell of the CSA was its attempt to kill FBI special agent Jack Knox , the lead agent who was assigned to investigate the group; Asa Hutchinson , the federal prosecutor; and the federal judge who presided over the affair that brought about the eventual action against Gordon Kahl , a tax protester and a member of the Posse Comitatus , by federal agents at CSA member Leonard Ginter 's home

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
descriptions: The CSA had links to The Order.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
descriptions: CSA is affiliated with The Order.
edges.evidence_quote: Affiliations: The Order , Aryan Nations , Elohim City

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord
descriptions: The CSA evolved from and succeeded the Zarephath-Horeb Community Church.
edges.evidence_quote: The CSA developed from a Baptist congregation, the Zarephath-Horeb Community Church, which was founded in 1971 in Pontiac, Missouri. Over time, Zarephath-Horeb evolved into an extremist militant group and it was rechristened the CSA.

Continentals.description: The Continentals were a Chinese American street gang based in New York City's Chinatown, founded in 1961 by American-born Chinese high school students for self-protection against other ethnic groups. They engaged primarily in non-violent crimes like vandalism and had up to 100 members. The gang was short-lived, active only until 1964, and is remembered for its less violent nature compared to later Chinese gangs.

descriptions: Flying Dragons succeeded the Continentals as a more violent gang in Chinatown.
edges.evidence_quote: In the late sixties with the 1965 immigration law, new gangs began to merge in Chinatown, succeeding the legacy left behind by the Continentals who were known for less violence in comparison to the new gangs that were to come like the Ghost Shadows and Flying Dragons that were significantly more violent and had the principal objective of racketeering.

descriptions: Ghost Shadows succeeded the Continentals as a more violent gang in Chinatown.
edges.evidence_quote: In the late sixties with the 1965 immigration law, new gangs began to merge in Chinatown, succeeding the legacy left behind by the Continentals who were known for less violence in comparison to the new gangs that were to come like the Ghost Shadows and Flying Dragons that were significantly more violent and had the principal objective of racketeering.

descriptions: The text contrasts the Continentals with Chung Ching Yee, noting that Chung Ching Yee recruited immigrants while Continentals could not.
edges.evidence_quote: After some time they had trouble recruiting new members due to the small number of American-born Chinese, whereas new gangs such as the White Eagles and Chung Ching Yee in California were able to recruit new members who were immigrants from Hong Kong and mainland China.

descriptions: The text contrasts the Continentals with White Eagles, noting that White Eagles recruited immigrants while Continentals could not.
edges.evidence_quote: After some time they had trouble recruiting new members due to the small number of American-born Chinese, whereas new gangs such as the White Eagles and Chung Ching Yee in California were able to recruit new members who were immigrants from Hong Kong and mainland China.

Artistas Asesinos.description: Mexican criminal organization founded in 2008 by Jorge Ernesto Sáenz and Éder Ángel Martínez, operating as an armed wing of the Sinaloa Cartel in Ciudad Juárez and the El Paso metropolitan area. Known for their artistic skills in drawing and graffiti, they engage in homicide, drug dealing, extortion, and robbery. Currently one of the main violent groups in Chihuahua, controlling part of the Cereso 3 prison and in conflict with rival groups.

edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Barrio Azteca

Black Guerrilla Family.description: African American black power prison gang, street gang, and political organization founded in 1966 by George Jackson, George 'Big Jake' Lewis, and W.L. Nolen at San Quentin State Prison in California. It is ideologically Marxist-Leninist and promotes black power and overthrowing the U.S. government. The group is involved in drug trafficking, burglary, and homicide, and remains active primarily in California and Maryland.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood struck against Black Guerrilla Family.
edges.evidence_quote: They decided to strike against the African-Americans who were forming their own militant group called the Black Guerrilla Family.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood engaged in a race war with Black Guerrilla Family in 1975.
edges.evidence_quote: The Aryan Brotherhood grew quickly in the California prison system and eventually engaged in a race war in 1975 with the other prison gangs such as La Nuestra Familia, and Black Guerilla Family.

descriptions: The Aryan Brotherhood is a rival of the Black Guerrilla Family.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood formed in response to Black Guerilla Family.

descriptions: Aryan Brotherhood attacked Black Guerrilla Family members.
edges.evidence_quote: Decidieron atacar a los afroamericanos que estaban formando su propio grupo militante llamado Black Guerrilla Family.
